q: A _____ capacitor is the simplest capacitor.
Charging a capacitor means connecting the capacitor to a _____.
The surface charge density on each plate is $\sigma$ = _____.
If the plates are very close together, the electric field between the plates is _____.
The value of the electric field between the plates can be expressed in terms of surface charge density $\sigma$ as $E$ = _____.
The magnitude of the potential difference $V$ between the plates equals _____.
The capacitance of the parallel plate capacitor is $C$ = _____.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is directly proportional to the _____ of its plates.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is inversely proportional to the _____ of the plates.
The permittivity $\epsilon$ of an insulating material is related to that of a vacuum $\epsilon_0$ as $\epsilon$ = _____.
Here $\kappa$ is the dielectric constant or the _____ of the insulating material.
The permittivity of vacuum $\epsilon_0$ is equal to _____ $\text{C}^2 \text{ N}^{-1} \text{ m}^{-2}$.
For a vacuum $\kappa$ = _____.
From Table 9.1, the dielectric constant of water ($18^\circ\text{C}$) is _____.
The dielectric constant of an insulating material is the ratio of its permittivity to that of a _____.
If there is a vacuum between its plates, its capacitance $C_0$ is _____.
Dielectric constant can be defined as the ratio of two _____.
When a charged capacitor is disconnected and air is replaced by an insulating material, the potential difference is found to _____.
When an insulating material is inserted into a disconnected charged capacitor, the _____ of the capacitor does not change.
Since the potential difference decreases and charge is constant, it indicates that the capacitance _____.
Dielectric constant can be expressed as the ratio of capacitances as well as the ratio of _____.
Insulating materials have values of dielectric constant $\kappa$ greater than _____.
A dielectric provides an increase in _____.
A dielectric provides an increase in maximum _____ voltage.
A dielectric provides possible _____ support between the plates.
Which of the following is NOT a type of capacitor mentioned in the text?
Tantalum and aluminum are types of _____ capacitors.
Smaller units of supercapacitors are used as power backup for _____ (SRAM).
Capacitors are used to eliminate _____ in automobile ignition systems.
Any flashing light or regular beeping uses a _____ capacitor.
If one conductor has a charge $+Q$ the other has a charge _____.
The potential difference between two conductors of the capacitor, $V$, is called the _____ of the capacitor.
The amount of charge given to a conductor to change its potential by one unit is called the _____ of the conductor.
Because the electric field between the plates is uniform, the _____ of the potential difference $V$ between the plates equals $E d$.
From Table 9.1, the dielectric constant of Air ($1\text{ atm}$) is _____.
The unit farad is named in honor of Michael _____.
Film capacitors are available in nearly any value and working voltages up to _____ $\text{V}$.
What does a dielectric increase even though the physical size of the capacitor does not change?
The dielectric may be able to withstand a larger what between the plates than air?
What is the standard value of the permittivity of free space $\varepsilon_0$ used in Example 9.2?
When the distance between the two parallel plates of a capacitor is doubled, by what percent does its capacitance change?
What happens to the capacitance when an insulating material is inserted between the conductors?
